name: Rate Law by Initial Rates
description: The method of initial rates determines a rate law empirically: a series of experiments varies one reactant concentration at a time while measuring the initial rate, the order in each reactant is read from how the rate scales, and once all orders are known the overall rate law is assembled and the rate constant is fitted.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Reaction of interest"]
    N2["Vary one [A], hold rest"]
    N3["Measure initial rate"]
    N4["Rate vs [A] dependence"]
    N5["Determine order in A"]
    N6{"All reactants done?"}
    N7["Vary next reactant"]
    N8["Assemble rate law"]
    N9["Fit rate constant k"]
    N10["Validated rate law"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|no| N7
    N7 -->|next| N2
    N6 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
